title: Connection<T...>​
content: </> interface Connection<T...> { Connected: boolean Disconnect: (Connection<T...>) → () Reconnect: (Connection<T...>) → () }

title: Disconnect​
content: </> Connection.Disconnect(self: Connection<T...>) → () Disconnects the connection from the signal. May be reconnected later using :Reconnect(). local signal = LemonSignal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(print, "Test") signal:Fire("Hello world!") -- Test: Hello world! connection:Disconnect() signal:Fire("Goodbye world!")

title: Reconnect​
content: </> Connection.Reconnect(self: Connection<T...>) → () Reconnects the connection to the signal again. If it's a :Once connection it will be disconnected after the next signal fire. local signal = LemonSignal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(print, "Test:") signal:Fire("Hello world!") -- Test: Hello world! connection:Disconnect() signal:Fire("Goodbye world!") connection:Reconnect() signal:Fire("Hello again!") -- Test: Hello again!

title: Signal​
content: </> interface Signal { Connect: ( Signal, fn: (...any) → (), T... ) → Connection<T...> Once: ( Signal, fn: (...any) → (), T... ) → Connection<T...> Fire: ( Signal, any ) → () DisconnectAll: (Signal) → () }

title: new​
content: </> Signal.new() → Signal Returns a new signal instance which can be used to connect functions. local LemonSignal = require(path.to.LemonSignal) local signal = LemonSignal.new()

title: Connect​
content: </> Signal.Connect( self: Signal, fn: (...any) → (), ...: T... ) → () Connects a function to the signal and returns the connection. Passed variadic arguments will always be prepended to fired arguments. local signal = LemonSignal.new() local connection1 = signal:Connect(function(str: string) print(str) end) local connection2 = signal:Connect(print, "Hello") signal:Fire("world!") -- Hello world! -- world!

title: Once​
content: </> Signal.Once( self: Signal, fn: (...any) → (), ...: T... ) → () Connects a function to a signal and disconnects if after the first fire. Can be reconnected later. local signal = Signal.new() local connection = signal:Once(print, "Test:") signal:Fire("Hello world!") -- Test: Hello world! print(connection.Connected) -- false

title: Fire​
content: </> Signal.Fire( self: Signal, ...: any ) → () Fires the signal and calls all connected functions with the connection's bound arguments and the fired arguments. local signal = LemonSignal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(print, "Test:") signal:Fire("Hello world!") -- Test: Hello world!

title: Wait​
content: </> Signal.Wait(self: Signal) → ...any Waits until the signal is fired and returns the fired arguments. local signal = LemonSignal.new() task.delay(1, function() signal:Fire("Hello", "world!") end) local str1, str2 = signal:Wait() print(str1) -- Hello print(str2) -- world!

title: DisconnectAll​
content: </> Signal.DisconnectAll(self: Signal) → () Disconnects all connections currently connected to the signal. They may be reconnected later. local signal = LemonSignal.new() local connection1 = signal:Connect(print, "Test1:") local connection2 = signal:Connect(print, "Test2:") signal:Fire("Hello world!") -- Test2: Hello world! -- Test1: Hello world! signal:DisconnectAll() signal:Fire("Goodbye World!")
